programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The Fifth Estate brings in-depth investigations important to Canadians, delivering a parade of political leaders, controversial characters and ordinary people with impacted lives.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: Uncovering wrongdoings in the marketplace; with the help of hidden cameras, correspondents dish out the dirt on not so "clean" business practices.

programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: Uncovering wrongdoings in the marketplace; with the help of hidden cameras, correspondents dish out the dirt on not so "clean" business practices.

programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The top-notch team of hosts Bob McKeown, Gillian Findlay, Mark Kelley and Habiba Nosheen provides unique and engaging documentaries from Canada and beyond. "The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: Canada's consumer watchdog series since 1972, this news program uncovers wrongdoings in the marketplace. With the help of hidden cameras, correspondents dish out the dirt on not so "clean" business practices. Once they get the evidence they need on tape, they apply pressure on businesses to change policies vis-a-vis the products and services they provide to the public.

programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: Canada's consumer watchdog series since 1972, this news program uncovers wrongdoings in the marketplace. With the help of hidden cameras, correspondents dish out the dirt on not so "clean" business practices. Once they get the evidence they need on tape, they apply pressure on businesses to change policies vis-a-vis the products and services they provide to the public.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

"The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

Once they get the evidence they need on tape, they apply pressure on businesses to change policies vis-a-vis the products and services they provide to the public.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The top-notch team of hosts Bob McKeown, Gillian Findlay, Mark Kelley and Habiba Nosheen provides unique and engaging documentaries from Canada and beyond. "The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Colombian born Martina Castillo is surprised with a culinary journey to discover her roots.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards reveals the surprising results of Jamila Doncher’s DNA test with a culinary journey.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern bakes up some delightful Italian classics.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host afternoon tea with Jessica McGovern’s nibble-worthy treats.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Baker Jessica McGovern shows how to get fancy with three stunning French desserts

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Indulge in a little comfort food as host Jessica McGovern creates warm and cozy desserts at home.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern bakes up three of her all-time favourite chocolatey recipes

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern creates three yummy desserts that include fruits and veggies.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern shows how to bake magic-in-the-middle goodies.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Baking is indulgent when making rich and delicious treats! Host Jessica McGovern shares some foolproof recipes for all things rich.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern offers tips and recipes to incorporate flowers in dessert!

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up Food Tuck inspired fare for the whole family in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Bring on the face wiping, finger licking goodness with messy and delicious, fall off the bone, soya beef ribs with sesame mayo.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer Watts is inspired to embrace big, big food for a big big barbecue feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Delicious grilled food is good for morning, noon and night, so let’s Brunch.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer’s feeling spicy! He makes a light chickpea salad with charred halloumi and a cumin vinaigrette.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: The sun is shining and Spencer is turning up the heat, cooking delicious grilled food perfect to be served on a bun.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer is inspired by all the fun different flavours you can bring to food with peppers.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Big platters of food, lots of smiling faces, sharing and laughter - who doesn't love a great grilled feast?

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Fire and meat has been done since the caveman era - but vegetarian dishes - that's where things can get really creative.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer celebrates his favourite vacation spot with a Hawaiian themed barbecued feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Indian and Asian spices are versatile and delicious, especially when you add a little char from the barbecue.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: The best grilled treats sometime come in bite sizes portions so Spencer is making grilled appetizers!

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer embraces the bounty of herbs available in the summer and makes lavender salted spatchcock chicken with a balsamic truffle glaze.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: A fiesta is a celebration and Spencer celebrates with some delicious grilled food!

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Making black bean and kabocha squash soup with braised Swiss chard and chili pesto.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer makes a spicy spinach and Taleggio cheese wonton soup in a chorizo broth.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: A pork bolognese stuffed tortellini with American cheese rose and soffrito.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Montreal smoked meat potato rosti; campfire steaks with a nori butter; sweet and creamy fondue.

programme.title: Comfort Food With Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Cajun shrimp cakes with a creole etouffee; Brazilian moqueca with halibut and shrimp.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Baking is indulgent when making rich and delicious treats! Host Jessica McGovern shares some foolproof recipes for all things rich.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Ride along on dramatic balloon flights into colorful geomagnetic storms to help revolutionize space travel.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Did Mars ever develop life? The search for answers reveals the forces that long ago doomed the Red Planet.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Examine the Universe's first micro-moments to learn why the Universe is vast almost beyond our comprehension.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: There are plans to send spacecraft to the Moon to find resources that may one day allow humans to live there.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: What are scientists learning about the history of the Moon and its relationship to the development of Earth?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Massive solar storms attack Earth by disrupting radio signals and navigation systems and harming power grids.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: Moon Shots
programme.desc: With rare archive footage, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Zero Gravity: Life on the International Space Station
programme.desc: ESA and NASA astronauts document six months exploring outer space at the International Space Station.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: Supermassive Black Hole at the Center of the Galaxy
programme.desc: The largest object in our galaxy is a supermassive black hole that dominates its central region.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: The surface of Venus is 95% carbon dioxide with an atmospheric pressure equivalent to 900 meters underwater.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: Surface temperatures reach 450 degrees - any technology we send to Venus wouldn't stand a chance in the heat.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Scientists are studying genomes of COVID-19 to track the disease's spread and develop lifesaving vaccines.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: The discovery of antibiotics has saved countless lives. But how do we stay one step ahead of "super-bugs"?

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Who can stop the menace caused by deadly mosquito-borne diseases? Mosquitoes kill a million people each year.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Finland is a country of geographic and cultural transition; at the same time with a very distinct identity.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Iceland is a volcanic island with a magnificent environment. Why did humans decide to colonize Iceland?

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: The Norwegian landscapes still bear traces of this tough geological history.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Sápmi becomes coastal along Norway, where it splinters off into thousands of islets.

programme.title: Elephant Refugees
programme.desc: When Botswana became poacher-free, a mass migration of elephants followed. It now is a haven for elephants.

programme.title: Rubber Jellyfish
programme.desc: Balloons' harmful effects, to us and to our environment, have been greenwashed and kept a secret for decades.

programme.title: Freaks of the Sea
programme.desc: In the oceans of our planet lurk creatures weirder than our wildest dreams and our scariest nightmares.

programme.title: Global Whale Shark Mystery
programme.desc: Despite its size, the greatest shark in the planet’s oceans, the whale shark, remains an enigma to scientists.

programme.title: City Slickers: Penguin Invasion
programme.desc: Boulders is a popular beach resort in South Africa, where penguins and people compete for habitat.

programme.title: The Wolf: Marine Mammal
programme.desc: British Columbian wolves are mastering fishing and swimming. Will they eventually evolve into marine mammals?

programme.title: The Lost Island of Madre de Dios
programme.desc: Watch divers, geologists, and biologists face extreme conditions to study this unexplored Antarctic land.

programme.title: Shark Cinema Science
programme.desc: This underseas journey explores advanced shark research worldwide with acclaimed filmmaker Andy Casagrande.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: Every year, a master shepherd takes 200 black-headed sheep through the middle of the German capital of Berlin.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In Kyrgyzstan, herders drive their herds into the mountains to pastures at nearly 2,500 meters (8,000 feet).

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In northeastern Uganda, a herder finds that a disease affecting cattle threatens his livelihood.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: A Raika camel herd has scattered throughout the dry forest of the Rajasthan land in northwest India.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.sub-title: Living with Alpacas in the Peruvian Andes
programme.desc: The rapidly changing climate threatens the way of life for an alpaca herder in the high Andes of Peru.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: The Kalahari: a thirsty land where brief rains spark vibrant life, soon reclaimed by the desert.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa’s southern coral reefs are vibrant and dynamic worlds in which remarkable creatures that live.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa's vast grasslands host epic wildlife gatherings, where survival depends on adapting to seasons.

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: Horses in Search of a King
programme.desc: A genetic anomaly threatens Przewalski’s wild horses. Can this rare species be saved from extinction?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: Golden Lion Tamarins narrowly escaped extinction in the 1970s, but will a new intruder spell the end?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: The Turtle Who Came Out of Nowhere
programme.desc: Scientists are approached by a previously undiscovered species of turtle. Does this new animal pose a threat?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: The oryx has been all but annihilated. Are these rare animals destined for extinction?

programme.title: The Lost Island of Madre de Dios
programme.desc: Watch divers, geologists, and biologists face extreme conditions to study this unexplored Antarctic land.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Wild Pacific - The Beauty of Life
programme.desc: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions. It extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean.

programme.title: Secrets of the North Sea
programme.desc: The North Sea is enigmatic. It presents itself in cool shades of grey. Yet who would have guessed that beneath the chilly waves are opulent coral gardens, courting squids, and several of the largest sea creatures on the planet?

programme.title: Mysterious Oceanic Oases - Hotspots of Life
programme.desc: Countless marine animals frolic on the steep slopes of underwater volcanoes. In many cases, several are rare species not found elsewhere. This is in part due to the fact that plankton clings to the wall and provides the fish with food in abundance. This documentary shows several of these oases and also examines the impact dive tourism has and whether preventive measures can help to maintain them. Nature and underwater filmmaker Rolf Möltgen undertakes dives into the mysterious underwater mountain landscapes with marine scientists from all corners of the globe. This journey takes us to Mexico, to snappers, white-tip reef sharks, Galapagos sharks and giant mantas. We visit the Maldives, Colombia and Costa Rica, where the team documents rare species such as deep sea burbots, small tooth sand tiger and dogfish sharks, in depths of more than 3 metres. The eerie underwater mountain world proves: everyone plays a part in the network of life. Thanks to consistent protective regulations, it has been possible to retain the underwater diversity - especially in the unique oases of the high seas

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: The contours of the Mediterranean have changed during the course of its history. Near Gibraltar, Africa was once connected to the European continent. When the land bridge at Gibraltar collapsed, the Mediterranean basin filled up again. The Suez Canal is today connected to the Red Sea. This route enables animals to reach the Mediterranean, where they cause problems.

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Rugged rocks, blue sea and idyllic, sandy beaches - this is the Mediterranean as we know it. But this impression is deceptive: the Mediterranean is under constant change. The continents are moving towards one another. Animals, that have to survive under such extreme conditions, must find a way to escape the dangers. Between deserts and volcanoes, the challenges for the animals are enormous.

programme.title: Europe's far North - Lofoten and the Arctic Ocean
programme.desc: Lofoten is the rugged archipelago in the Norwegian Arctic Ocean. Whoever arrives here is really at the end. Moskenes is the end of the line, and anyone who wants to go further needs a boat or a plane. The only way to get to the open sea is via the Maelstrom, which is quite dangerous. On the outermost headland, there are only a few ruins left. The last inhabitants are long gone, a supply was hardly possible. But fishermen are on the way, mainly to catch cod. And there is the coastal administration, which takes care of broken sea marks and lighthouses. A visit to one of the luxurious ships of the Hurtig Line is not to be missed. They shuttle off rough coasts on an eleven-day trip between the Russian border and southern Norway. A floating workplace for a wide variety of professions. The island of Andøya is not served by the Hurtiglinie. It lies too far out in the Atlantic. One of the most important observatories in Europe is located here. A young woman, Sandra Blindheim, is the boss. She is responsible for the large laser that delivers important information to scientists around the world. On clear winter nights, there is a wonderful panoramic view of the aurora borealis.

programme.title: Jacques Cousteau's Legacy - Return To The Undersea World
programme.desc: A composition of wildlife footage, archive material and elaborate re-enactments results in a brilliant reconstruction of the life of famed marine biologist and environmental pioneer Jacques Cousteau. On the trail of the most famous oceanographer of the 20th Century, the team spent two years filming original locations on three different continents, in four different oceans. The film charts how Cousteau’s outlook on the environment changed throughout his many decades at sea around the globe. On his personal journey to enlightenment, he changed from a man who killed sharks in hate and thoughtlessly blew up coral reefs, to a radical environmentalist.

programme.title: Realm of the robber - Christmas Island
programme.desc: Thievery and treachery are second nature for the world's largest land crab. This documentary explores the untold story of Christmas Island's iconic robber crabs. Armed with claws that deliver more force than a lion's bite, they can grow a metre across and are relentless in their search for food. However, the brash crustaceans have surprisingly complex private lives and often find themselves in precarious situations, whether escaping from frenzied ants or risking their life to deposit fertilised eggs into a raging ocean. To tell the story of the robber crab's life cycle, directors Moritz Katz and Braydon Moloney spent a year in the field, following their subjects in minute detail. The tragedies, trials and triumphs paint a fresh, humorous portrait of Christmas Island and its nefarious denizens.

programme.title: Lobsters - The Knights Of The Deep
programme.desc: The knights of the deep are on the move; almost no part of their bodies is without armor. Like medieval warriors, these fighters are well armed – not with sword and shield, but with scissors and forceps and defensive armor that is covered with spikes and hooks. And even though they are well protected and have an uncanny awareness of their surroundings, many of these proud knights will face a gloomy destiny. Many of them will end upon a plate. Gourmets all over the world are in love with these famous crustaceans: lobsters! For marine biologists lobsters are similarly fascinating: as research objects. We are on their trail in Cape Breton, an island in the northern Atlantic and part of the Canadian province Nova Scotia, where one of the largest populations of lobsters has its home.

programme.title: Vicious Beauties - The secret World of the Jelly Fish
programme.desc: Watching the graceful movement of these gelatinous animals is almost hypnotic. Although their bodies are 98% water, jellyfish are deceptively clever and dangerous. But where do these primordial creatures come from? How do they reproduce and how long do they live?

programme.title: Predators of a Different Kind - In the World of the Unknown Sharks
programme.desc: With the aid of modern technology, reveal the unknown behaviour of some unusual species of shark: lemon sharks and their white-tip reef counterparts. Our film trip begins on the Bahamas. To be more precise: in the turquoise-blue waters of the Grand Bahama Bank, in an exactly fixed location. In the spring, 70 – 80 pregnant lemon sharks arrive here. This huge shark population was first discovered just a few years ago by shark researcher, Professor Sam Gruber. Many of the females are tired and rest on the seabed. Pregnant tiger sharks swim in the midst of this group. Without any protection whatsoever, our cameramen shoot their footage, surrounded by sharks and succeed in capturing images hitherto unseen. We continue our journey to Gainesville, Florida, to meet Gordon Hubbell, the leading shark denture expert with the world’s largest shark denture collection. He knows everything about the evolutionary history of the lemon sharks, in addition to those of the white-tip reef variety off Cocos Island, Costa Rica. Here, we encounter large schools of fish, unprecedented numbers of stingrays, as well as hammerhead sharks. However, it is the white-tip reek sharks that make the biggest impression. Their performance begins late at night. Marauding, they patrol in large groups through the reefs and hunt everything that moves. Scales and dead prey fish float above the reef – a welcoming change of diet for the ubiquitous barracudas.

programme.title: Giants of Fortune - The Whale Sharks of Oslob
programme.desc: In the little Filipino village of Oslob on the Cebu Island, people suffer from typhoons and overfishing. Until the world’s biggest fish and the Internet change everything.Fisherman Zosimo’s dearest wish is for his children to graduate from high school one day. Yet the chance that this wish will come true is tiny, since at times Zosimo doesn’t even manage to feed the family. Typhoons and overfishing make the lives of Oslob fishermen difficult.

programme.title: The Oceans Seven - The Marathon Swimming Challenge
programme.desc: André Wiersig, born in 1972, has achieved what no German has ever done before: he has swum through the world's seven most famous straits, the so-called Ocean's Seven. To prepare for it, he chose a familiar but hostile environment. In his carport, he hardened himself in a rain barrel filled with groundwater.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Mauritius
programme.desc: Renaud Van Der Meeren is a photographer and paramotor pilot. With his motorised parachute he goes on expeditions to the remotest corners on earth to photograph people and landscapes from the air. Renaud wanted to be a jet pilot, studied music at the Sorbonne, became a ski instructor, model and photographer's assistant before he found happiness in his current vocation. He especially likes to do his job in Mauritius – nowhere else does he find so many motifs as on the "sweetest pearl in the Indian Ocean". This time he's working on a photo book about Mauritius – by land, by water and in the air.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Sao Tome and Principe
programme.desc: Along with photographer Rui Camilo, we travel to the West African island nation of Sao Tomé and Príncipe - in search of images that will capture the soul of this small and beautiful country.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Cape Verde
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: mareTV: Martinique
programme.desc: The island of Martinique is a piece of France in the Caribbean, between Florida and South America. And of course, Savoir-vivre applies just as it does on the other side of the Atlantic, between the English Channel and the Mediterranean. Jacky Pascault is the first and only man to cultivate mushrooms in the Caribbean. Everywhere else, they grow on artificially fertilised soil, but Jacky's Champignons Tropicaux flourish on sugar cane fibres. Nicole Vilo tailors hats and dresses out of coconut palms. Of course, the catwalk for the models wearing her first collection is one of the countless dream beaches. The Martiniquais sail on boats they call Yole, which have no keel and are therefore difficult to navigate. The crew members hang over the water in breakneck fashion on long wooden poles. Just the job for Yole-boss Max' "problem kids": they not only learn complicated handling, but also team spirit.

programme.title: mareTV: Curacao, Blue Wonder of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Curacao is one of the most popular attractions for countless cruise ships on their trips through the Caribbean, because it is located outside of the hurricane belt. This is why Eric Miguel is constantly in action. He is the bridge keeper of the Queen Emma Bridge, the landmark of Curacao's capital, Willemstad. After his shift, Eric goes to Yvonne Troeman's cook-shop. Her speciality is Kadushi, a cactus soup. The ingredients include cactus flesh, pigtail's, mussels and a few secret "treats". Barbara van Bebber is the only submarine pilot on Curacao and literally gets to the bottom of things amidst the turquoise-blue wonder of the Caribbean. She charters out her sub to marine biologists and wealthy private explorers, simultaneously cleaning up on the seabed. Her "Curasub" has claw arms, which she employs to remove anything that doesn't belong down there: beer bottles, car tyres, and plastic refuse. Jeroen Eikelenboom simply carts sand from a neighbouring cove to his own surf club in his favourite bay.

programme.title: mareTV: Mexico's Caribbean Coast
programme.desc: The palm tree laden beaches on Mexico's Riviera Maya on the Yucatan peninsula are endless, and the turquoise sea with its gentle Caribbean surf is a warm 25 degrees during the mild winter in this part of the world. Between Cancún and Punta Allen, where once there was little more than bushland and a few sleepy fishing villages, vibrant life has evolved during the past three decades. Richard Sowa has lived on a floating island for the past 15 years: an island he built himself out of empty plastic bottles. The bon vivant him-self sees it as a "solution for our planet's refuse problem". Off Punta Allen, a village in the bay of Sian Ka'an, Mexican Mara Coral Ley and her cousin Kim, continue to catch lobsters by adhering to the traditional method and were awarded an "Eco seal for sustainable fishing" for their trouble. It is silent under water off Isla Mujeres and it is here that the artist Jason deCaires has constructed a sculpture park. Hundreds of lifesize figures form an artificial reef at a depth of 12 metres. Fish and plants complete his work of art. The master himself is convinced: "Mother Nature is the true artist".

programme.title: Caribbean sisters: The Lesser Antilles St. Kitts & Nevis
programme.desc: In the centre of the Caribbean, volcanoes rise steeply from the sea, thus shaping the sister islands of St. Kitts & Nevis. Their slopes, covered in lush rainforests, snow-white, sandy beaches and the surrounding tur-quoise tinted waters provide the ultimate setting for this record-breaking, albeit tiny island paradise: Both islets are the smallest of the Caribbean dwarf states and also rank among the world’s ten smallest states. A hot pepper sauce, made of a creative mixture of ginger, garlic, thyme and extraordinarily hot chillies that thrive in the hot and humid climate of Nevis, is simmering on the stove of Violet and Llewellyn’s house. Lemmy Pemberton digs up abandoned turtle nests with his bare hands, as he wants to be certain that all of the babies have indeed managed to make it. Members of the Turtle Group catch sea turtles in order to take their measurements and fit them with transmitters. Taxi bus-es are not only the main means of transport on St. Kitts & Nevis, they are also an artistic expression of each driver’s personality. Colourfully embellished, each taxi represents island art on wheels.

programme.title: Cuba - Brilliantly Caribbean
programme.desc: Even anyone who has not yet been to Cuba can conjure up certain images of American vintage street cruisers, imposing buildings that have seen better days, white sandy beaches full of palms and loving couples on the Malecón against a backdrop of crashing waves. This island is somewhat of a myth and its people generate a special joie de vivre. The legendary Hershey Train departs for Matanzas from the capital, Havana. On the oldest electrified railway line in the Caribbean, 60-year-old Spanish carriages make their way along al-most 100 kilometres of coastline in the most relaxed way possible. The Salvavidas on Varadero, probably the most famous beach on the island, have a particular-ly beautiful workplace. The lifeguards' territory ex-tends for more than 20 kilometres. In order to protect the coasts of Cuba, mangroves have been replanted here for many years. The project is supported by the UN and planting is often carried out by schoolchildren. In doing so, Cuban children learn how to treat their maritime habitat in a sustainable fashion. The former colonial city of Trinidad on Cuba's south coast is gen-erally considered to be one of the most beautiful places in the entire Caribbean. The houses here are particular-ly colourful. A plethora of magnificent buildings can be seen at the Plaza Mayor, the neo-baroque main square.

programme.title: Chandani - The Daughter Of The Elephant Whisperer
programme.desc: Chandani, a girl in a male profession, takes on the role of a Mahout, supported by ranger Mohammed

programme.title: Saliega's Lineage, The Return Of The Iberian Lynx
programme.desc: The Iberian lynx was saved by captive females San Diego and Ora, and their newborns.

programme.title: The Unknown Snake Of Bolivia
programme.desc: Herpetologists Juan Tims and Hiram Cuevas explore Bolivia to find venomous snakes and a new viper.

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Dolphins have fascinated humans for centuries, sparking curiosity about the bond between humans and dolphins.

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Scientists are shifting focus to ""how intelligent are dolphins?"" using research from top experts.

programme.title: The Gentle Bear Man of Emo
programme.desc: Michael's connection with wild animals on his Emo property helped him heal his soul after a tragic event

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Bears, owls, and tigers use sharp claws for hunting, while baboons, wolves, and woodchucks use their teeth

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Take a look at various animals fighting styles such as elks, gorillas, Kangaroos and shrimp.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog, and penguin benefit from coloration

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.

programme.title: Sperm Whales: Dealing with the Unexpected
programme.desc: The Sperm Whale family in the Crozet Islands faces threats from humans, fishing lines, and deep-sea monsters.

programme.title: Trees Of Life
programme.desc: See how humble plants triggered Earth’s greatest transformation

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Dolphins have fascinated humans for centuries, sparking curiosity about the bond between humans and dolphins.

programme.title: Conversations With Dolphins
programme.desc: Scientists are shifting focus to ""how intelligent are dolphins?"" using research from top experts.

programme.title: The Gentle Bear Man of Emo
programme.desc: Michael's connection with wild animals on his Emo property helped him heal his soul after a tragic event

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Bears, owls, and tigers use sharp claws for hunting, while baboons, wolves, and woodchucks use their teeth

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Take a look at various animals fighting styles such as elks, gorillas, Kangaroos and shrimp.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog, and penguin benefit from coloration

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.
